GaAgas. In my view Talikot is preferable.
Girinagara is the modern Junagadh, which is immortalized by
the Girnar inscription of Rudradaman I.
Bharukaccha is the modern Broach which was Barygaza to
the Greeks.
Malaya and Dardura are twin mountains in the south. The

Division of Globe XIV 173
former stands for the southern part of the western Ghats from the
Nilgiris to Cape Gomorin. In the Mahabharata (Drona 23.70-72)
it is associated with the Pandyas. Dardura is identified with the
Nilgiris or Palni Hills. Kalidasa compares these two mountains to
the bosoms of Earth
Mahendra 4'embraces the entire chain of hills extending from
Ganjam to Tinnevelly.,, Kalidasa associates it with Kalinga
(Raghu. VI. 64). In the Rsmayana Hanuman is described as flying
across the ocean from the summit of this mountain.
Abhlras are supposed to have entered India from eastern Iran
about the second century B.C. They were also considered to be
Sudras.
